articles.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
articles.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

en.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
en.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

de.title: Der Geheimagent, der UFO-Geheimnisse enthüllen wollte, starb vor fünf Jahren. Ein Whistleblower hat nun Verdacht geäußert
de.summary: Kevin Childress, ein 30-jähriger Kriminalermittler des Energieministeriums, starb 2021 unerwartet in seinem Haus in Georgia, wobei die Behörden Komplikationen durch COVID-19 anführten. UFO-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo behauptet, Childress sei vor seinem Tod gesund gewesen und habe gewarnt, dass das DOE eine wichtige Rolle in UAP-Programmen gespielt habe. Elizondo sagt, das Energieministerium versuche ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen und stellt fest, dass weder eine öffentliche Autopsie noch eine detaillierte Todesursache veröffentlicht wurden.

articles.title: Ukrainian Defence Ministry Advisor Drops Bizarre UFOs Encountered By Military Amid Pentagon's Disclosure [WATCH]
articles.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, a senior adviser to Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, revealed that the Ukrainian Armed Forces have an official program to track and study unidentified flying objects, a directive approved by the Commander‑in‑Chief and active since the early days of the war with Russia. He said the program is a national‑security necessity because unidentified aerial activity over Ukrainian territory could represent new Russian weapons technology, and he posted 2023 reconnaissance footage as evidence.

en.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, a senior adviser to Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, revealed that the Ukrainian Armed Forces have an official program to track and study unidentified flying objects, a directive approved by the Commander‑in‑Chief and active since the early days of the war with Russia. He said the program is a national‑security necessity because unidentified aerial activity over Ukrainian territory could represent new Russian weapons technology, and he posted 2023 reconnaissance footage as evidence.

de.title: Berater des ukrainischen Verteidigungsministeriums lässt bizarre UFOs fallen, auf die das Militär gestoßen ist, nachdem das Pentagon dies offengelegt hat [ANSCHAUEN]
de.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, ein leitender Berater des ukrainischen Verteidigungsministers, gab bekannt, dass die ukrainischen Streitkräfte über ein offizielles Programm zur Verfolgung und Untersuchung nicht identifizierter Flugobjekte verfügen, eine vom Oberbefehlshaber genehmigte und seit den Anfängen des Krieges mit Russland aktive Richtlinie. Er sagte, das Programm sei eine Notwendigkeit für die nationale Sicherheit, da nicht identifizierte Luftaktivitäten über ukrainischem Territorium eine neue russische Waffentechnologie darstellen könnten, und er veröffentlichte Aufklärungsaufnahmen aus dem Jahr 2023 als Beweis.

articles.title: UFO files | In pursuit of pies in the sky
articles.summary: S. Department of War released 162 declassified UAP documents, photos and videos under President Donald Trump, with further updates planned through the PURSUE program that involves the White House, NASA, FBI, ODNI and AARO. The files contain fuzzy dots, a four‑pointed star and two enhanced photos of smears from Apollo 12 and 17, but 108 documents are heavily redacted and four were retracted, with the Pentagon citing protection of eyewitness identities, facility locations and sensitive military information.

en.title: UFO files | In pursuit of pies in the sky
en.summary: S. Department of War released 162 declassified UAP documents, photos and videos under President Donald Trump, with further updates planned through the PURSUE program that involves the White House, NASA, FBI, ODNI and AARO. The files contain fuzzy dots, a four‑pointed star and two enhanced photos of smears from Apollo 12 and 17, but 108 documents are heavily redacted and four were retracted, with the Pentagon citing protection of eyewitness identities, facility locations and sensitive military information.

de.title: UFO-Dateien | Auf der Suche nach Kuchen im Himmel
de.summary: Das US-Kriegsministerium veröffentlichte unter Präsident Donald Trump 162 freigegebene UAP-Dokumente, Fotos und Videos. Weitere Aktualisierungen sind im Rahmen des PURSUE-Programms geplant, an dem das Weiße Haus, die NASA, das FBI, ODNI und AARO beteiligt sind. Die Dateien enthalten unscharfe Punkte, einen vierzackigen Stern und zwei verbesserte Fotos von Abstrichen von Apollo 12 und 17, aber 108 Dokumente sind stark redigiert und vier wurden zurückgezogen, wobei das Pentagon den Schutz von Augenzeugenidentitäten, Standorten von Einrichtungen und sensiblen militärischen Informationen anführt.

en.title: A small Oregon town puts on a big UFO festival. Why it's worth a visit
en.summary: S. after Roswell. The festival, which began in 2000 to celebrate the 1950 McMinnville UFO sighting and photos, features speakers, live music, a parade, costume contests, and a vendor street fair, with tickets required for most speaker presentations.

de.title: Eine kleine Stadt in Oregon veranstaltet ein großes UFO-Festival. Warum es einen Besuch wert ist
de.summary: S. nach Roswell. Das Festival, das im Jahr 2000 begann, um die UFO-Sichtung und Fotos von McMinnville im Jahr 1950 zu feiern, bietet Redner, Live-Musik, eine Parade, Kostümwettbewerbe und einen Straßenmarkt mit Händlern. Für die meisten Rednerpräsentationen sind Tickets erforderlich.

summaries.file-release-bright-lights-and-hot-orbs-: The U.S. government released 17 pages of previously classified Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena documents on May 8, as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, with more files to be issued on a rolling basis. Florida has reported 8,872 UFO sightings since 1995, the second‑highest total in the nation, and the state has been the site of several notable incidents, including a 1944 P‑38 aircraft encounter, a 1967 Cuban Jet Incident involving radar operators, the 1987‑88 Gulf Breeze Six photographs, the 2012 Homestead Golden Knights event, a 2018 satellite image over Pratt & Whitney, and a 2021 Flagler County video. Many of these sightings have been later explained or debunked: the Gulf Breeze photos were shown to be a hoax involving a foam model, the Golden Knights were identified as a parachutist team using flares, and the Pratt & Whitney image was likely a stealth drone or test rig. Pentagon AARO director Sean Kirkpatrick and NASA official Dan Evans have stated that, to date, no credible evidence of extraterrestrial activity or off‑world technology has been found in UAP reports. The remaining uncertainty lies in the interpretation of the released documents and the unexplained nature of some sightings.
summaries.film-how-to-watch-sleeping-dog-online-st: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.
summaries.secret-agent-who-was-about-to-blow-the-l: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released. The FBI is investigating a series of missing and dead scientists linked to secret nuclear and UFO programs, including the disappearance of retired General William Neil McCasland. Other scientists, such as David Wilcock, have also died under suspicious circumstances, adding to the pattern of unexplained losses.
summaries.program-ukrainian-defence-ministry-advis: Serhii Beskrestnov, a senior adviser to Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, revealed that the Ukrainian Armed Forces have an official program to track and study unidentified flying objects, a directive approved by the Commander‑in‑Chief and active since the early days of the war with Russia. He said the program is a national‑security necessity because unidentified aerial activity over Ukrainian territory could represent new Russian weapons technology, and he posted 2023 reconnaissance footage as evidence. Beskrestnov noted that a formal UAF document governs the study of such phenomena and that the state institution handling the investigations has been active since the Soviet era. He urged military personnel and civilians who capture UFOs on camera to submit footage to uap@gur.gov.ua, arguing that field‑level visual evidence could be valuable intelligence. His statements came as the U.S. Pentagon declassified millions of UFO records, with the Department of War releasing 162 files on May 8 2026, 158 of which are now available online.
summaries.ufo-files-in-pursuit-of-pies-in-the-sky: On May 8 the U.S. Department of War released 162 declassified UAP documents, photos and videos under President Donald Trump, with further updates planned through the PURSUE program that involves the White House, NASA, FBI, ODNI and AARO. The files contain fuzzy dots, a four‑pointed star and two enhanced photos of smears from Apollo 12 and 17, but 108 documents are heavily redacted and four were retracted, with the Pentagon citing protection of eyewitness identities, facility locations and sensitive military information. The collection also includes videos of unidentified aerial phenomena from Greece, Iraq, the UAE and a U.S. Indo‑Pacific Command sighting near Japan, as well as an FBI‑Lab rendering of an ellipsoid shape reported in 2023. AARO’s 2024 report revealed hundreds of new UAP sightings but no confirmation of alien technology, and a second report is due soon, though expectations for new revelations remain low. The release drew over 340 million hits on war.gov/UFO in the first half‑day, prompting critics to view it as a deflection tactic rather than substantive disclosure.
summaries.a-small-oregon-town-puts-on-a-big-ufo-fe: The 26th Annual McMenamins UFO Festival in McMinnville, Oregon, runs from May 14 to 17 and is hosted by the Hotel Oregon, making it the second‑largest UFO festival in the U.S. after Roswell. The festival, which began in 2000 to celebrate the 1950 McMinnville UFO sighting and photos, features speakers, live music, a parade, costume contests, and a vendor street fair, with tickets required for most speaker presentations. Organizers say the event brings skeptics and believers together, and this year’s festival coincides with the release of new federal “UFO files,” adding fresh material for discussion. Most activities are free, but tickets can be purchased at ufofest.com, and the festival is held in the downtown historic district at 310 NE Evans St.

de.title: So schauen Sie sich „Sleeping Dog“ online an – streamen Sie die umstrittene UAP/UFO-Dokumentation von überall aus
de.summary: Der Journalist und Filmemacher Jeremy Corbell bespricht seinen neuen Dokumentarfilm mit NBC News-Korrespondent Gadi Schwartz. Der Dokumentarfilm trägt den Titel „Sleeping Dog“. Es untersucht Corbells Umgang mit Regierungsgeheimnissen. Es geht auch um Whistleblower und seine Suche nach der Wahrheit über UFOs.

en.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
en.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

de.title: Der Geheimagent, der UFO-Geheimnisse enthüllen wollte, starb vor fünf Jahren. Ein Whistleblower hat nun Verdacht geäußert
de.summary: Kevin Childress, ein 30-jähriger Kriminalermittler des Energieministeriums, starb 2021 unerwartet in seinem Haus in Georgia, wobei die Behörden Komplikationen durch COVID-19 anführten. UFO-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo behauptet, Childress sei vor seinem Tod gesund gewesen und habe gewarnt, dass das DOE eine wichtige Rolle in UAP-Programmen gespielt habe. Elizondo sagt, das Energieministerium versuche ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen und stellt fest, dass weder eine öffentliche Autopsie noch eine detaillierte Todesursache veröffentlicht wurden.

en.title: Ukrainian Defence Ministry Advisor Drops Bizarre UFOs Encountered By Military Amid Pentagon's Disclosure [WATCH]
en.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, a senior adviser to Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, revealed that the Ukrainian Armed Forces have an official program to track and study unidentified flying objects, a directive approved by the Commander‑in‑Chief and active since the early days of the war with Russia. He said the program is a national‑security necessity because unidentified aerial activity over Ukrainian territory could represent new Russian weapons technology, and he posted 2023 reconnaissance footage as evidence.

de.title: Berater des ukrainischen Verteidigungsministeriums lässt bizarre UFOs fallen, auf die das Militär gestoßen ist, nachdem das Pentagon dies offengelegt hat [ANSCHAUEN]
de.summary: Serhii Beskrestnov, ein leitender Berater des ukrainischen Verteidigungsministers, gab bekannt, dass die ukrainischen Streitkräfte über ein offizielles Programm zur Verfolgung und Untersuchung nicht identifizierter Flugobjekte verfügen, eine vom Oberbefehlshaber genehmigte und seit den Anfängen des Krieges mit Russland aktive Richtlinie. Er sagte, das Programm sei eine Notwendigkeit für die nationale Sicherheit, da nicht identifizierte Luftaktivitäten über ukrainischem Territorium eine neue russische Waffentechnologie darstellen könnten, und er veröffentlichte Aufklärungsaufnahmen aus dem Jahr 2023 als Beweis.

en.title: UFO files | In pursuit of pies in the sky
en.summary: S. Department of War released 162 declassified UAP documents, photos and videos under President Donald Trump, with further updates planned through the PURSUE program that involves the White House, NASA, FBI, ODNI and AARO. The files contain fuzzy dots, a four‑pointed star and two enhanced photos of smears from Apollo 12 and 17, but 108 documents are heavily redacted and four were retracted, with the Pentagon citing protection of eyewitness identities, facility locations and sensitive military information.

de.title: UFO-Dateien | Auf der Suche nach Kuchen im Himmel
de.summary: Das US-Kriegsministerium veröffentlichte unter Präsident Donald Trump 162 freigegebene UAP-Dokumente, Fotos und Videos. Weitere Aktualisierungen sind im Rahmen des PURSUE-Programms geplant, an dem das Weiße Haus, die NASA, das FBI, ODNI und AARO beteiligt sind. Die Dateien enthalten unscharfe Punkte, einen vierzackigen Stern und zwei verbesserte Fotos von Abstrichen von Apollo 12 und 17, aber 108 Dokumente sind stark redigiert und vier wurden zurückgezogen, wobei das Pentagon den Schutz von Augenzeugenidentitäten, Standorten von Einrichtungen und sensiblen militärischen Informationen anführt.

en.title: A small Oregon town puts on a big UFO festival. Why it's worth a visit
en.summary: S. after Roswell. The festival, which began in 2000 to celebrate the 1950 McMinnville UFO sighting and photos, features speakers, live music, a parade, costume contests, and a vendor street fair, with tickets required for most speaker presentations.

de.title: Eine kleine Stadt in Oregon veranstaltet ein großes UFO-Festival. Warum es einen Besuch wert ist
de.summary: S. nach Roswell. Das Festival, das im Jahr 2000 begann, um die UFO-Sichtung und Fotos von McMinnville im Jahr 1950 zu feiern, bietet Redner, Live-Musik, eine Parade, Kostümwettbewerbe und einen Straßenmarkt mit Händlern. Für die meisten Rednerpräsentationen sind Tickets erforderlich.
